program_en: B.Sc. in Food and Nutritional Sciences
program_chi: 食品及營養科學理學士
learning_outcomes:
1 Major Programme:
Upon completing the programme, students will have
Knowledge outcomes
(1)  |  a scientific foundation in food and nutritional sciences
adequate to begin working effectively in the field.
(2)  |  an understanding of fundamental concepts and up-to-date
issues in food and nutritional sciences.
(3)  |  an understanding of how to apply the knowledge in the
food and nutritional sciences to practical situations in the food and
nutritional sciences.
Skills outcomes
(1)  |  the ability to access, retrieve and critically evaluate
relevant food science/nutritional science information.
(2)  |  the ability to make judgments about alternative
perspectives and consider different points of view.
(3)  |  the ability to explore new things in a multi-disciplinary
way.
(4)  |  the ability to engage in self-directed learning.
(5)  |  the ability to use knowledge to solve problems in food
and nutritional sciences.
(6)  |  the ability to communicate effectively with a wide range
of people in speaking and in writing.
(7)  |  the ability to work as an effective team or group member.

to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 59[a] units of courses as follows:

(ii)  |  FNSC4902 and 4903
(iii) (iv) (v)  |  FNSC4906 and/or LSCI4000# LSCI4911# and 4912# and 4913# LSCI4912# and 4913#

3333, 3520#, MBTE4320#, 4530#, STAT3210#
Total:  |  59[a]
Students admitted to the Food and
Nutritional Sciences through the broad-based admission scheme may apply
to specialize in the Nutritionist Stream by the end of second term in
their second year of attendance. Students applying to specialize in this
stream must have obtained (1) a GPA of 3.3 or above; (2) an overall
result of at least 6.5 in IELTS with no less than 6.0 in each component; (3) at least a C in FNSC2005; and (4) recommendation from the selection
panel. The minimum unit requirement for the Nutritionist Stream is 66.
Nutritionist Stream
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 66 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
Group

A
course from the following Group C: MATH1520 (preferred)
or 1010 Group D: (PHYS1001 or 1002) (preferred) or 1111 Group
E: STAT1012 (preferred) or 1011

BCHE2030#, BIOL2120#, 2410#, FNSC2001, 2002, 2005, 3001,
3002, 3010, 3030, 3110, 4001, 4002, 4101, 4102, 4110, 4120, 4150, 4160,
4170, LSCI2002#
3.  |  Capstone Courses:  |  4
FNSC4906, 4907
Total:  |  66
In addition to fulfilling the above Major Programme
Requirement, students meeting the criteria as specified by the Faculty
can take the following stream offered by the Faculty:
Science, Technology And
Research Stream
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 12 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required
Courses:
(a)  |  One Faculty Package Course:  |  3
Choose from the two remaining groups of the Faculty
Package that have not been used to fulfill the Faculty Package
Requirement
(b)  |  Research Courses:  |  6
STAR2000, 3000, 4000[b]
(c)  |  Seminar Courses:  |  3
STAR2050, 3050, 4050
2.  |  Experiential Learning:  |  ---
At least 4 consecutive
weeks of outside Hong Kong exposure[c]
Total:  |  12
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  FNSC courses at 2000 and above level as
well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
[a]  |  Study Option: Berkeley Biosciences
Study Abroad Programme Students who undergo one term of
studies at the University of California, Berkeley, USA will take courses
offered by the Berkeley Biosciences Study Abroad Programme. Units obtained
from the Programme will be recognized as up to 12 units of Major
Programme requirement. This option will be recorded on the transcript.
[b]  |  Students may select research-oriented course(s), as
approved by the Major Programme, to substitute up to 4 units for the fulfillment
of Research Courses requirement.
[c]  |  Students must complete any
exchange/research/internship programme(s) offered by the University,
Colleges, the Faculty of Science or Major Programme, as approved by the
Major Programme, to fulfill the Experiential Learning requirement.
Students are responsible for the extra costs incurred in the exchange/research/internship
programme(s).
